free_text: A neutron elastic scattering study of Chromium, Iron
and Nickel in the energy region 1.77 to 2.76 MeV
Studsvik 6 MeV van de Graaff with a
beam pulse width at half maximum  2 nanosec.
Natural, cylinder having length of 5.0 cm, 0.95 cm
inner diam. and 2.5 cm outer diam. for Cr,Fe,Ni.
Polythene scatterer,3.0 cm in length,0.95 cm outer
Scattering samples are positioned with their symmetry
axes perpendicular to that of the target
scintillator NE102A, 10 cm diameter and 5 cm length;
the neutron flux was monitored with a

Total elastic scattering cross-sections calculated by
Hauser-Feshbach theory with and without correction for
width-fluctuation agree with the experimental data
The experimental data were corrected for attenuation,
anisotropy of the neutron flux in the scatterer,
multiple elastic scattering, and for the finite
geometries by a Monte Carlo program.
